title: Jesse Sene-Lefao

Career Stats & Summary|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2015-10-11}} | updated = 5 January 2023 | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Matthew Wright (rugby league)|Matthew Wright]] (cousin)
[[Tim Simona]] (uncle) }} }} '''Jesse Sene-Lefao''' (born 8 December 1989) is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. Sene-Lefao has previously played for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] and the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in the [[NRL]], plus [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. ==Background== Sene-Lefao was born in [[Wellington]], New Zealand. ==Early years== Sene-Lefao is the nephew of [[West Tigers]] centre [[Tim Simona]], and cousin of [[North Queensland Cowboys]] centre [[Matthew Wright (rugby league)|Matthew Wright]]. Sene-Lefao is of [[Samoan people|Samoan]] descent. Sene-Lefao played his junior football for the [[Brothers Penrith|Penrith Brothers]] before being signed by the [[Penrith Panthers]]. Sene-Lefao played for the Panthers' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2008 and 2009, scoring 15 tries in 38 games.{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/s/sene-lefao_jesse/index.php|access-date=4 February 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090715100205/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/s/sene-lefao_jesse/index.php|archive-date=15 July 2009|title=Harness and Greyhound}} ==Playing career== ===2013=== In [[2013 NRL season|2013]], Sene-Lefao joined the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]]. He made his [[NRL]] debut in the opening round of the season against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] at [[Suncorp Stadium]], playing off the interchange bench in Manly-Warringah's 22–14 win.{{cite web|url=http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news-display/Final-Team-List-Rd1-Sea-Eagles-v-Broncos/69567 |title=Final Team List: Rd1 Sea Eagles v Broncos |access-date=4 February 2016 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130505044943/http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news-display/Final-Team-List-Rd1-Sea-Eagles-v-Broncos/69567 |archive-date=5 May 2013}}{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/broncos-v-sea-eagles-preview/tabid/10874/newsid/70992/default.aspx|title=Broncos v Sea Eagles preview|publisher=NRL.com|date=2013-03-06|access-date=2015-10-11}} Sene-Lefao played his 2nd and last match for the year in round 8.{{cite web|author=James Polson|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/rugby-league-match-centre/live-st-george-illawarra-dragons-v-manly-sea-eagles-20130506-2j3au.html|title=Live: St George Illawarra Dragons v Manly Sea Eagles|publisher=Smh.com.au|date=2013-05-06|access-date=2015-10-11}} [[File:Jesse Sene-Lefao.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Sene-Lefao playing for the Manly Sea Eagles in 2013]] Sene-Lefao spent most of the year playing for the team's [[New South Wales Cup]] team. ===2014=== Sene-Lefao was selected in Manly's inaugural [[2014 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad.{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=Squads For Auckland Nines|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-02-14|access-date=2015-10-11|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archive-date=2016-01-15}} He made his international debut in May for [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the [[Pacific Rugby League International#2014 test|test]] against [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] at [[Penrith Stadium]]. Sene-Lefao played off the interchange bench in Samoa's 32–16 win.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/gutsy-samoa-secures-place-in-four-nations-tournament-following-win-over-fiji/story-fni3fbgz-1226904268873|title=Gutsy Samoa Secures Place in Four Nations Tournament|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-10-11|date=2014-05-03}} Soon after, Sene-Lefao re-signed with the Manly-Warringah club on a two-year contract.{{cite web|url=http://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2014/06/26/sea_eagles_re_sign_sene_lefao.html|title=re-sign Sene-Lefao|publisher=Sea Eagles|date=2014-06-26|access-date=2015-10-11}}{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/manly-re-sign-sene-lefao/|title=Manly Re-Sign Sene-Lefao|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2014-06-27|access-date=2015-10-11|archive-date=22 October 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141022080046/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/manly-re-sign-sene-lefao/|url-status=dead}} In round 13 against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] at [[Brookvale Oval]], Sene-Lefao scored his first NRL career try in Manly's 32–10 win.{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/manly-loses-daly-cherryevans-as-sea-eagles-soar-against-canterbury-bulldogs/story-fni3gnk1-1226946017521|title=Manly Loses Daly Cherry-Evans as Sea Eagles Soar Against Canterbury Bulldogs|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-10-11|date=2014-06-06}} It was the sole try he scored from 25 matches in [[2014 NRL season|2014]]. At the end of the season, Sene-Lefao was a late selection for Samoa in the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] after [[Suaia Matagi]] was selected in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] squad.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/sene-lefao-leaves-bucks-night-for-test-team/tabid/10874/newsid/82567/default.aspx|title=Sene-Lefao leaves bucks night for Test team|publisher=NRL.com|date=2014-10-17|access-date=2015-10-11}} ===2015=== Sene-Lefao was again part of the Manly [[2015 Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]] squad in 2015.{{cite web |url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/sea-eagles-unveil-2015-nines-squad/ |title=SEA EAGLES UNVEIL 2015 NINES SQUAD | Rugby League Week |access-date=2015-09-27 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150716054235/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/sea-eagles-unveil-2015-nines-squad/ |archive-date=2015-07-16 }} He scored two tries from his 11 matches in [[2015 NRL season|2015]]. On 9 October, Sene-Lefao signed a two/year contract with the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], starting from 2016 after he was released from the final year of his contract with Manly.{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/jesse-sene-lefao-joins-cronulla/tabid/10874/newsid/90492/default.aspx|title=Jesse Sene-Lefao joins Cronulla|publisher=NRL.com|date=2015-10-09|access-date=2015-10-11}} ===2016=== Sene-Lefao played 6 games for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in 2016. He was a vital squad member for Cronulla-Sutherland in 2016, filling in for captain Paul Gallen during the semis, and the injured Sam Tagataese towards the end of the season. Although he did not play in Cronulla's 2016 drought breaking premiership win. On 21 October 2016, Sene-Lefao signed a two-year contract with [[Castleford Tigers]].{{cite web |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/10624349/jesse-sene-lefao-signs-for-castleford-on-two-year-contract |title=Jesse Sene-Lefao signs for Castleford on two-year contract |publisher=Sky Sports |date=20 October 2016 |access-date=17 January 2023}} ===2017=== Sene-Lefao played in the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2017/oct/07/castleford-v-leeds-grand-final-2017-live-super-league-rugby?page=with:block-59d9128de4b00593b4d67a05#liveblog-navigation|title=Castleford 6-24 Leeds: Grand Final 2017 – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=7 October 2017|access-date=6 November 2019}}{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/41536394|title=Grand Final 2017: Castleford 6-24 Leeds Rhinos|date=7 October 2017|work=BBC Sport|access-date=6 November 2019}}{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2017/oct/07/castleford-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Danny McGuire guides Leeds to Grand Final success over Castleford|date=7 October 2017|work=Guardian|access-date=6 November 2019}} ===2018=== In the [[2018 Super League season]], he made 17 appearances for Castleford as the club finished third on the table but failed to reach the grand final.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/super-league/table|title=Tables – Rugby League|website=BBC Sport}} ===2019=== Sene-Lefao played 24 games for Castleford as they finished the [[Super League XXIV]] season in 5th place on the table. Castleford reached the second week of the 2019 Super League finals series where they were defeated by Salford 22-0 in the elimination semi-final.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/salford-vs-castleford/live/52308/|title=Salford Red Devils 22-0 Castleford Tigers recap|website=Sky Sports}} ===2020=== Sene-Lefao made nine appearances for Castleford in the [[2020 Super League season]]. The club finished a disappointing ninth on the table, their lowest finish since 2013.{{cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1790/season-review-castleford-tigers/|title=Season Review Castleford|website=www.superleague.co.uk}} ===2021=== On 17 July 2021, Sene-Lefao played for Castleford in their [[2021 Challenge Cup Final]] loss against St. Helens.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390|title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} ===2022=== On 20 December 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]]{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Dec 2021|accessdate=21 Dec 2021|title=Jesse Sene-Lefao given starting number by Featherstone|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jesse-sene-lefao-given-starting-number-by-featherstone/}} On 28 May, he played for Featherstone in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final loss against [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}} On 31 October, he signed a two-year deal to join fellow RFL Championship side [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]] ahead of the 2023 season.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jesse-sene-lefao-finds-new-club-in-the-championship/|title=Jesse Sene-Lefao finds new club in the Championship|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}} == Statistics == {| class="wikitable" |+ !Year !Team !Games !Tries !Pts |- |2013 | rowspan="3" |{{Leagueicon|Manly|16}}
